stemHtml:
The mass and balance information gives:
Basic Empty Mass: 1700 kg
BEM CG: 4.00 m
Under these conditions the Basic centre of gravity is at 27% of the Mean Aerodynamic Chord. The length of MAC is 2.00 m. In the mass and balance section of the flight manual, the following information is given:

Position arm front seats: 2.7 m
Position arm rear seats: 3.6 m
Position arm rear hold: 4.9 m
Position arm Fuel tanks: 3.2 m

The pilot and one passenger embark; each weighs 85 kg. The fuel tanks contain 160 liters of 100LL with a density of 0.72. The rear seats are not occupied. Taxi fuel is negligible.
What is the position of the centre of gravity in percentage of Mean Aerodynamic Chord at take-off?
